_doc: Canned responses extracted from the original investigation's raw/ CLI outputs (scrubbed). Substring dispatch, first match wins — specific rules precede generic ones.

rules._doc: Full log set (Info + Error). Also serves --level Error queries — the Error entry carries the full exception stack either way.

Alternate trace-retrieval form (platform traces command). Same span payload as `or jobs traces` — without this rule the call falls through to the empty unmocked_default and the agent concludes no upstream activity existed for the wrong reason (degraded replicate observed on a sibling scenario).

Faulted-only job list recorded from the original session. Placed before the generic jobs-list rules; other flag orderings fall through to the full job list, whose newest entry is the same faulted job.
Fallback for flag-order variations. The tenant has one folder of interest (Shared); the recorded folder-scoped job list is the correct answer for any jobs-list variant the agent tries.
Per CLAUDE.md: docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs and canned responses go stale. Cached per-query within a run.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any uip command not matched by `rules` returns an empty array. Lets the agent explore beyond the recorded path without aborting.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.
Agent must fetch the failing job to read the raw NullReferenceException from ForwardMail.ExecuteAsync.

Trace spans carry the binding evidence (ForwardMail Message='originalEmail', To=non-null literal, only 3 spans / no producer activity); the agent may retrieve them via `or jobs traces <key>` or `traces spans get --job-key <key>`.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions (Pending -> Running -> Faulted) confirm a clean single execution.
